title: quercus alnifolia
intro: Quercus alnifolia, also known as the Alpine oak or Korean white oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Quercus alnifolia has been found to have several health benefits, such as lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Quercus alnifolia include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
